headline: Trump's Iran Blockade Spikes Oil, Nasdaq Slumps; Fed Warns on Rates, SK Hynix Plunges
summary: US markets saw volatility as President Trump reinstated an Iran port blockade and proposed a 20% charge on Hormuz cargo, driving oil prices sharply higher. Fed Governor Waller warned that persistent core inflation could necessitate further rate hikes, increasing market uncertainty. SK Hynix experienced a significant sell-off following its Nasdaq debut, impacting the broader memory chip sector.

bullets.lead: Trump reinstates Iran port blockade and proposes Hormuz transit fees.

segments.text: The US President's move includes a 20% charge on cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions and impacting global oil markets.

sources.title: Trump reinstates Iran port blockade and vows 20% charge on cargo passing through Hormuz
Trump Restarts Iran Blockade as US Plans 20% Hormuz Charge

segments.text: This policy shift follows Trump's earlier decision to cut nearly 3 million acres from two Utah national monuments.

sources.title: Trump cuts nearly 3 million acres from 2 Utah national monuments

bullets.lead: Oil prices surge on renewed US-Iran conflict fears.

segments.text: Crude futures saw their largest jump since 2020, reflecting market bets that the Strait of Hormuz will not return to normal operations.

sources.title: Oil Jumps as Trump Vows to Renew Iran Blockade, Nasdaq Slumps | Markets P.M. for July 13
Oil jumps as Trump claims new Hormuz blockade on Iran

segments.text: The renewed blockade and potential fees could imply a cost of $30 million per supertanker, raising concerns about global supply chains.

sources.title: Trump’s Hormuz Demand Implies Fee of $30 Million Per Supertanker

bullets.lead: Fed's Waller warns of potential rate hikes if inflation persists.

segments.text: Governor Christopher Waller stated the Fed should not "fight the last war" on inflation but cautioned that rate hikes might still be necessary if core inflation remains elevated.

sources.title: Waller says Fed shouldn't 'fight the last war' on inflation but warns hikes still possible
Fed's Waller says rate hike may be needed if core inflation stays hot
The Fed’s Christopher Waller Warns of Higher Rates Soon if Inflation Stays High
Top Fed official warns ‘hot’ inflation could trigger rate rise

segments.text: This hawkish stance has led to rising odds for a July rate hike from the Federal Reserve.

sources.title: A July rate hike from the Fed? The odds are rising

bullets.lead: Treasury two-year yield rises to highest since 2025.

segments.text: The yield increase reflects market concerns over persistent inflation and the potential for further monetary tightening.

sources.title: Treasury Two-Year Yield Rises to Highest Since 2025 on Oil Gain

segments.text: India's inflation accelerated to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts, adding to global inflation pressures.

sources.title: India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts

bullets.lead: Google explores Samsung for next-gen AI chip production.

segments.text: The tech giant is reportedly considering Samsung for partial production of its 10th-generation TPU, 'Icefish,' with TSMC handling the core compute engine on 1.4nm and Samsung potentially producing memory I/O dies on 2nm.

sources.title: Exclusive: Behind Google’s TPU Ground War to Lure Nvidia’s Most Loyal Customers

segments.text: This move aims to diversify supply amid tight TSMC capacity due to high Nvidia AI chip demand.

sources.title: Exclusive: Behind Google’s TPU Ground War to Lure Nvidia’s Most Loyal Customers

bullets.lead: TSMC's June 2026 revenue surges 68% on AI demand.

segments.text: The strong performance highlights continued robust demand for AI chips, with TSMC also planning single-digit price hikes for mature-process chip manufacturing in Q4.

sources.title: TSMC June 2026 revenue surges 68% as AI demand continues to fuel growth

segments.text: Nvidia's upcoming Rubin V platform is reportedly facing a PCB bottleneck due to issues with Victory Giant's HDI PCBs, which could delay resolution.

bullets.lead: SK Hynix reportedly begins HBM4 shipments to Nvidia.

segments.text: This development positions SK Hynix as a key supplier for next-generation high-bandwidth memory, with full ramp-up expected by September.

sources.title: SK Hynix reportedly starts Nvidia HBM4 shipments ahead of September ramp

segments.text: Samsung is also actively recruiting for HBM development, targeting HBM5, and developing glass interposers as TSMC expands packaging capacity.

sources.title: Samsung develops glass interposer as TSMC expands packaging capacity
삼성, HBM 개발직 전방위 채용...HBM5 목표로 인력 확보 속도전

bullets.lead: SK Hynix shares plunge after Nasdaq debut.

segments.text: The company's ADRs tumbled significantly following a sharp sell-off in Korea, with the stock falling 15% on "Black Monday" in Seoul and its US ADRs dropping 9.3%.

sources.title: SK하이닉스 15% 폭락 '검은 월요일'…美 ADR도 9.3% 급락
SK Hynix plunges after Nasdaq debut as memory chip euphoria cools
SK Hynix ADRs Tumble in Second Trading Day After Korea Selloff
Stocks making the biggest moves midday: Nio, Braiin, AppLovin, SpaceX, SK Hynix & more

segments.text: This decline occurred despite some venture capital funds expressing no concern over SK Hynix's fall.

sources.title: [부자들의 투자노트] 美 벤처캐피털 펀드 "SK하이닉스 하락 걱정 안 돼"

bullets.lead: Samsung accelerates HBM development and fab operations.

segments.text: Samsung is aggressively hiring for HBM development, aiming for HBM5, and has accelerated the start of operations for its first fab in the Yongin semiconductor cluster by two years, now targeting October 2029.

sources.title: 삼성, HBM 개발직 전방위 채용...HBM5 목표로 인력 확보 속도전

bullets.lead: Meta expands Louisiana data center capacity to 5 gigawatts.

segments.text: The company's investment in its Louisiana data center now crosses $50 billion, doubling its compute capacity, with Broadcom as a chief design partner and TSMC manufacturing its 'Iris' AI chip.

sources.title: Meta expands Louisiana data center to 5 gigawatts, investment crosses $50 billion
Meta Doubles Louisiana Data Center Capacity

bullets.lead: Apple reportedly sues OpenAI for trade secret theft.

segments.text: Apple has allegedly accused OpenAI of encouraging employees to share confidential information related to upcoming Apple products.

sources.title: Apple Suing OpenAI for Stealing Trade Secrets, Former Meta Director of AI on Muse Spark — TITV [Video]

segments.text: OpenAI is also reportedly preparing to launch its first consumer AI device, potentially a smart speaker or wearable pin, as early as February.

watchlist.note: Watch for further developments on US-Iran tensions and their impact on global crude supply and prices.

sources.title: TSMC June 2026 revenue surges 68% as AI demand continues to fuel growth

x_themes.topic: Meta's 'Iris' AI chip production and compute expansion
x_themes.summary: Meta plans to produce its 'Iris' AI chip from September, aiming to double compute to 14GW next year, with Broadcom as chief design partner and TSMC manufacturing. Meta has also secured long-term supply agreements with Samsung (memory), SanDisk (flash storage), and Sumitomo (fiber optic).

x_themes.topic: Apple sues OpenAI for trade secret theft
x_themes.summary: Apple has reportedly sued OpenAI for allegedly stealing trade secrets related to upcoming Apple products, accusing OpenAI of encouraging employees to share confidential information for its own device development.

x_themes.topic: Nvidia Rubin V PCB bottleneck and Victory Giant issues
x_themes.summary: Nvidia's Rubin V is facing a PCB bottleneck due to Victory Giant's HDI PCB issues, specifically an impedance mismatch degrading signal integrity, which could delay resolution until late July or later.

An analysis argues that AI is not a bubble, contrasting current tech investment and profitability trends with the dot-com era's diverging investment and eroding profit shares, while also addressing valuation, market concentration, and circular financing concerns.

x_themes.topic: Apple's accelerated M-series chip roadmap for AI
x_themes.summary: Apple is expected to launch a base M6 this fall, skip Pro/Max/Ultra, and move directly to M7 in H1 2027, accelerating due to significant Neural Engine AI upgrades, with M7 Ultra supporting up to 1.5TB memory and M8 chips planned for 2028 on 1.4nm.

x_themes.topic: TSMC vs. Samsung in CPO and advanced packaging
x_themes.summary: TSMC is noted as leading in switch CPO, while Samsung is advancing its 2.xD packaging, which integrates HBM, logic, and silicon photonics on an interposer, aiming for a design win binding these components within twelve months.
TSMC plans to hike prices on mature-process chip manufacturing by a single-digit percent in Q4, effective January, driven by AI boom demand for chips like PMICs.

x_themes.topic: OpenAI's planned consumer AI device launch
x_themes.summary: OpenAI's first consumer AI device, potentially a smart speaker, smart glasses, or wearable pin, could launch as early as February, though its timeline might be disrupted by the ongoing dispute with Apple.

x_themes.topic: Nanya Technology's 2027 capex for new DRAM fab
x_themes.summary: Nanya Technology plans to spend NT$200 billion (US$6.2 billion) on 2027 capex, aiming for 30,000 wpm capacity by 2028 at its new fab, part of a NT$480B project.

x_themes.topic: Samsung accelerates Yongin semiconductor cluster fab operations
x_themes.summary: Samsung has accelerated the start of operations for the first fab in its Yongin semiconductor cluster by two years, now targeting October 2029 for the first of six planned fabs.

x_themes.topic: IBK Research report on Boston Dynamics humanoid robotics value chain
x_themes.summary: An IBK Research report maps suppliers for Boston Dynamics' Atlas robot (Hwashin, LG Energy, Hyundai Autoever, Mobis) and models humanoid volume ramps, highlighting actuator capacity as a key indicator for 2028-2030 growth.

x_themes.topic: Low-DK Gen 2 PCB demand surge and resilient pricing
x_themes.summary: Demand for Low-DK Gen 2 PCBs is surging starting 2H26 due to new GPU and ASIC chips, leading to incredibly resilient pricing and potential further increases despite supply chain expansion challenges and tough yield issues.

x_themes.topic: CPO system constraints and timeline drivers
x_themes.summary: CPO's volume ramp is constrained by energy, heat, and reach, with its timeline dependent on the readiness of 800V power, liquid cooling, external-laser reliability, and optical-engine qualification, highlighting the interplay of light, electricity, and heat.

x_themes.topic: SK Hynix ADR performance and potential Samsung ADR listing
x_themes.summary: SK Hynix ADR ($SKYHV) is up 16% from listing, suggesting investors may have prepared funds for the US instrument, with expectations for the Korean market to rally and questions arising about a potential Samsung Electronics ADR listing.
